tests.name: parseTryOnRequest parses a valid url-based request and applies the default category
parseTryOnRequest accepts a base64 person image
parseTryOnRequest accepts params with seed, garmentImage and numSamples
parseTryOnRequest leaves params undefined when omitted
parseTryOnRequest rejects a non-integer seed
parseTryOnRequest rejects numSamples = 0 (boundary: just-under min 1)
parseTryOnRequest accepts numSamples = 1 (boundary: at min)
parseTryOnRequest accepts numSamples = 4 (boundary: at max)
parseTryOnRequest rejects numSamples = 5 (boundary: just-over max 4)
parseTryOnRequest throws when tenantId is missing
parseTryOnRequest throws when shopperId is missing
parseTryOnRequest throws when personImage is missing
parseTryOnRequest throws when productId is missing
parseTryOnRequest throws on empty tenantId / shopperId / productId (boundary: min length 1)
parseTryOnRequest accepts single-character ids (boundary: exactly min length 1)
parseTryOnRequest rejects an unsupported category value
parseTryOnRequest rejects a non-https person image url
parseTryOnRequest safeParse reports the failing path for a missing tenantId
parseTryOnRequest rejects a non-object input
parseTryOnRequest property: any well-formed request parses and round-trips through the schema
parseTryOnResult parses a valid result
parseTryOnResult accepts cached results with zero cost
parseTryOnResult rejects a negative latencyMs (boundary: just-under 0)
parseTryOnResult accepts latencyMs = 0 (boundary: at 0)
parseTryOnResult rejects a negative costUsd (boundary: just-under 0)
parseTryOnResult rejects http / file / javascript result urls (fail-closed scheme allow-list)
parseTryOnResult rejects a bare "https://" with no host (boundary: malformed https url)
parseTryOnResult accepts an inline data:image/svg+xml base64 url (renderable offline result)
parseTryOnResult accepts inline data: urls for every allow-listed raster MIME type
parseTryOnResult rejects data: urls of disallowed mime types (text/html, image/gif)
parseTryOnResult rejects a data:image url with an empty / non-base64 payload (boundary)
parseTryOnResult accepts a data url exactly at the size cap and rejects one just over it (boundary)
parseTryOnResult throws when provider is empty
parseTryOnResult safeParse fails for a missing cached flag
parseImageRef — url variant accepts a well-formed https url
parseImageRef — url variant rejects an http (non-https) url
parseImageRef — url variant rejects a data: url even if it claims an image mime
parseImageRef — url variant rejects a file: url
parseImageRef — url variant rejects a malformed url string
parseImageRef — url variant rejects a url variant that carries base64 fields (discriminated union strictness)
parseImageRef — url variant property: any https url with a host parses; the same url over http is rejected
parseImageRef — base64 variant accepts a small base64 png
parseImageRef — base64 variant accepts every allow-listed mime type
parseImageRef — base64 variant rejects a non-allow-listed mime type (image/gif)
parseImageRef — base64 variant rejects empty data (boundary: min length 1)
parseImageRef — base64 variant rejects non-base64 characters
parseImageRef — base64 variant accepts a payload exactly at the max decoded size (boundary: at limit)
parseImageRef — base64 variant rejects a payload one byte over the max decoded size (boundary: just-over)
parseImageRef — base64 variant accepts a payload one byte under the max decoded size (boundary: just-under)
parseImageRef — discriminator rejects an unknown kind
parseImageRef — discriminator rejects a missing kind
parseImageRef — discriminator rejects a non-object input
httpStatusForErrorCode maps each documented code to its specific status
httpStatusForErrorCode is total: every ErrorCode maps to a 4xx or 5xx status
httpStatusForErrorCode fails closed to 500 for a runtime code outside the union
httpStatusForErrorCode the static mapping has exactly one entry per enum option
httpStatusForErrorCode property: every code in the enum resolves to a 4xx/5xx (totality, no gaps)
makeApiError derives httpStatus from the code so they cannot disagree
makeApiError produces a value that passes ApiError validation for every code
parseApiError parses a valid api error
parseApiError rejects an unknown code
parseApiError rejects an empty message (boundary: min length 1)
parseApiError rejects httpStatus = 399 (boundary: just-under min 400)
parseApiError accepts httpStatus = 400 and 599 (boundaries: at min/max)
parseApiError rejects httpStatus = 600 (boundary: just-over max 599)
parseApiError rejects a non-integer httpStatus
parseProviderId accepts every known provider id
parseProviderId rejects an unknown provider id
parseProviderId safeParseProviderId reports success for known and failure for unknown ids
parseProviderConfig parses a valid config
parseProviderConfig accepts a negative priority (ordering may use any integer)
parseProviderConfig rejects a non-integer priority
parseProviderConfig rejects a negative costPerCallUsd (boundary: just-under 0)
parseProviderConfig accepts costPerCallUsd = 0 (boundary: at 0)
parseProviderConfig rejects timeoutMs = 0 (boundary: just-under min 1)
parseProviderConfig accepts timeoutMs = 1 (boundary: at min)
parseProviderConfig accepts timeoutMs = 300000 (boundary: at max)
parseProviderConfig rejects timeoutMs = 300001 (boundary: just-over max)
parseProviderConfig rejects maxConcurrency = 0 (boundary: just-under min 1)
parseProviderConfig accepts maxConcurrency = 1 (boundary: at min)
parseProviderConfig rejects maxConcurrency = 1001 (boundary: just-over max 1000)
parseProviderConfig throws when id is unknown
parseProviderConfig safeParse fails for a missing enabled flag
parseProviderConfig property: timeoutMs strictly inside [1, 300000] always parses
parseTryOnJob parses a valid queued job (request defaults applied)
parseTryOnJob accepts every status value
parseTryOnJob rejects an unknown status
parseTryOnJob accepts a succeeded job carrying a result
parseTryOnJob accepts a failed job carrying an error
parseTryOnJob rejects an empty error string (boundary: min length 1)
parseTryOnJob leaves result, error and idempotencyKey undefined when omitted
parseTryOnJob accepts an optional idempotencyKey
parseTryOnJob rejects an empty idempotencyKey (boundary: min length 1)
parseTryOnJob rejects a non-ISO createdAt datetime
parseTryOnJob throws when jobId is missing
parseTryOnJob throws when the embedded request is invalid
parseTryOnJob safeParse fails for a missing updatedAt
parseTenantConfig parses a valid config and defaults killSwitch to false
parseTenantConfig preserves an explicit killSwitch = true
parseTenantConfig accepts an empty allowedProviders list (deny-by-default)
parseTenantConfig rejects an unknown provider in allowedProviders
parseTenantConfig rejects perShopperPerMinute = 0 (boundary: just-under min 1)
parseTenantConfig accepts perShopperPerMinute = 1 (boundary: at min)
parseTenantConfig rejects a non-integer rate limit
parseTenantConfig rejects a negative monthlyBudgetUsd (boundary: just-under 0)
parseTenantConfig accepts monthlyBudgetUsd = 0 (boundary: at 0)
parseTenantConfig rejects a negative retentionSeconds (boundary: just-under 0)
parseTenantConfig rejects a non-integer retentionSeconds
parseTenantConfig throws when tenantId is missing
parseTenantConfig safeParse fails for a missing rateLimit
parseAuditEvent parses a valid allow event
parseAuditEvent accepts every outcome value
parseAuditEvent rejects an unknown outcome
parseAuditEvent accepts optional provider and costUsd
parseAuditEvent leaves provider and costUsd undefined when omitted
parseAuditEvent rejects an unknown provider
parseAuditEvent rejects a negative costUsd (boundary: just-under 0)
parseAuditEvent accepts costUsd = 0 (boundary: at 0)
parseAuditEvent rejects a non-ISO ts
parseAuditEvent throws when each required field is missing
parseAuditEvent rejects an empty action string (boundary: min length 1)
parseAuditEvent safeParse fails and reports the path for a missing requestId
@tryit/contracts barrel re-exports the schema, type-parse, and helper surface from every module
@tryit/contracts barrel the re-exported parser actually validates (end-to-end through the barrel)
images base64 guard — start anchor (mutation-hardening) rejects a payload with a junk prefix followed by valid base64 (anchored ^)
images base64 guard — start anchor (mutation-hardening) rejects leading-whitespace + valid base64 (anchor must pin the start)
images base64 guard — start anchor (mutation-hardening) still accepts a clean base64 payload (no false positive from the anchor)
isAcceptableResultImageUrl — start anchor (mutation-hardening) rejects a javascript-scheme prefix in front of a valid data-url tail (anchored ^)
isAcceptableResultImageUrl — start anchor (mutation-hardening) rejects arbitrary leading text before a valid data-url tail
isAcceptableResultImageUrl — start anchor (mutation-hardening) still accepts a clean data:image url (no false positive from the anchor)
